mechanism_narrative: Higher trailing 3-month growth in retail sales signals stronger near-term household demand and revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 362294.0 as of one month before the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or has Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 4.5% marks restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and tightens credit conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% after being at or below 4.5% in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 4.49 as of one month before the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances have nonnegative average 60-day SPX returns or if negative hit rate is below 55% across at least 20 triggered walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in payroll employment signals a deterioration in labor income and recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month change is below 0
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.8 as of one month before the decision point
fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 134789.0 as of one month before the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive average 20-day SPX returns or if the interaction term between rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth turning positive after contraction signals improving factory output and earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth crosses above 0 after being at or below 0 in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 110.269 as of two months before the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth crossings above zero are followed by nonpositive mean 60-day SPX returns or if the signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices combined with rising policy rates signals an inflation-tightening mix that compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth exceeds its prior 12-month average and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is greater than 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 199.0 as of two months before the decision point
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 4.49 as of one month before the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase condition is followed by positive average 20-day SPX returns or if either component alone explains all predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction demand and tighter household credit transmission, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 2276.0 as of two months before the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines below the trigger are followed by positive mean 60-day SPX returns or if the negative hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment while policy rates are not rising signals improving labor income without an immediate tightening offset, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive average 5-day SPX returns or if the positive labor effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is stable or declining.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth signals broad demand contraction and downward earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 11247.6 as of five months before the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth crossings below zero are followed by positive average 60-day SPX returns or if recession-like GDP contractions fail to lower forward returns versus baseline.
